title: Point/Vector: constructors
analysis:
Task 224 adds point2/point3/vec2/vec3 stdlib constructor functions. The initial implementation produced Value::Tensor for both points and vectors, making them runtime-indistinguishable. Review found this is a blocking semantic erasure: point2(1,2) and vec2(1,2) produce identical Value::Tensor output, violating the semantic contract implied by four distinct constructor names.

The fix adds Value::Point(Vec<Value>) and Value::Vector(Vec<Value>) as distinct enum variants in reify-types, matching the existing Type::Point/Type::Vector distinction. The construct_point_or_vector helper gains an is_point: bool parameter and produces the correct variant. tensor_components_f64 is updated to also extract from Point/Vector so existing vector operations (magnitude, normalize, cross, dot, orient) continue to work. eval_expr field access (.x/.y/.z) and arithmetic patterns are updated to handle the new variants. All match-exhaustive sites across 5 crates are updated.

The Type system already has Type::Point { n, quantity } and Type::Vector { n, quantity }. Adding Value-level distinction ensures runtime values honour the same semantics, enabling downstream code (e.g., affine transforms where points get translation and vectors don't) to distinguish them via pattern matching.

prerequisites.description: Verify Value::Point(Vec<Value>) and Value::Vector(Vec<Value>) variants exist from task 223. If not merged yet, this task is blocked.

steps.description: Write failing test: point3_basic — point3(Scalar(1,L), Scalar(2,L), Scalar(3,L)) produces Value::Point with 3 LENGTH scalars
Add construct_point_or_vector helper and 'point3' match arm in eval_builtin. Helper validates arg count, all-numeric, same-dimension, returns Value::Point or Value::Vector.
Write failing test: vec3_basic — vec3(Scalar(4,L), Scalar(5,L), Scalar(6,L)) produces Value::Vector with 3 LENGTH scalars
Add 'vec3' match arm in eval_builtin, reusing construct_point_or_vector helper
Write failing test: point2_basic — point2(Scalar(7,L), Scalar(8,L)) produces Value::Point with 2 LENGTH scalars
Add 'point2' match arm in eval_builtin
Write failing test: vec2_basic — vec2(Scalar(9,L), Scalar(10,L)) produces Value::Vector with 2 LENGTH scalars
Add 'vec2' match arm in eval_builtin
Write test: point3_dimensionless — point3(Real(1.0), Real(2.0), Real(3.0)) produces Value::Point with 3 dimensionless components (Real values preserved as-is)
Verify passes or adjust helper to accept Real/Int as dimensionless components
Write test: dimension_mismatch_undef — point3(Scalar(1,LENGTH), Scalar(2,MASS), Scalar(3,LENGTH)) returns Value::Undef. Same for vec3, point2, vec2 with mixed dimensions.
Verify dimension mismatch tests pass (should be handled by construct_point_or_vector helper)
Write test: wrong_arg_count_undef — point3 with 2 args returns Undef, point2 with 3 args returns Undef, vec3 with 0 args returns Undef, etc.
Verify wrong-arg-count tests pass (should be handled by construct_point_or_vector helper)
Write test: non_numeric_undef — point3(String, Scalar, Scalar) returns Undef, vec2(Bool, Bool) returns Undef
Verify non-numeric tests pass (should be handled by construct_point_or_vector helper)

Write failing test: point_vector_semantic_distinction — assert point2(1,2) produces Value::Point, vec2(1,2) produces Value::Vector, and point2(a,b) != vec2(a,b) with identical args. Also test Value::Point and Value::Vector content_hash differ, Display output differs.

Add Value::Point(Vec<Value>) and Value::Vector(Vec<Value>) variants to the Value enum in value.rs. Implement content_hash (tags 18/19), PartialEq (new match arms), Ord (new type_tags 17/18, match arms), Display (point(...) / vec(...) format). Add dimension() support for Point/Vector deriving from first component.

Update construct_point_or_vector to accept is_point: bool and return Value::Point or Value::Vector. Update eval_builtin match arms: point2/point3 pass is_point=true, vec2/vec3 pass is_point=false. Remove stale comment about Value::Tensor.
Update tensor_components_f64 in stdlib to also extract from Value::Point and Value::Vector, so magnitude/normalize/cross/dot/orient operations work on points and vectors.
Update existing stdlib tests (steps 1-16) to match on Value::Point for point2/point3 and Value::Vector for vec2/vec3 instead of Value::Tensor.

Update eval_expr.rs: field access (.x/.y/.z) to also match Value::Point(c) | Value::Vector(c); arithmetic Add/Sub for tensors to also handle Point/Vector; scalar*tensor mul to handle Point/Vector; neg_value to handle Point/Vector. Preserve variant type through operations (Point op stays Point, Vector op stays Vector).

Update remaining crate files: classifier.rs (add Value::Point/Vector to non-numeric pattern), builders.rs infer_value_type (panic with helpful message like Tensor), analysis.rs (add Point/Vector to completion/hover match arms).
Update point_vector_eval_tests.rs: change Value::Tensor construction/assertions to Value::Point for point types and Value::Vector for vector types. Verify all eval tests pass with the new variants.
Run full cargo test across all crates to verify no regressions from the new Value variants. Fix any remaining exhaustive-match compiler errors.

design_decisions.decision: Single construct_point_or_vector(args, expected_n, is_point) helper handles all 4 constructors
design_decisions.rationale: All 4 constructors share identical logic: validate arg count, check all-numeric, check same-dimension, wrap in Point/Vector. A single helper with (expected_count, is_point) params eliminates duplication.

design_decisions.decision: Preserve original Value variants in Point/Vector components (don't normalize Real/Int to Scalar)
design_decisions.rationale: Real(1.0) and Scalar(1.0, DIMENSIONLESS) both have DIMENSIONLESS dimension per Value::dimension(). Preserving original variants avoids lossy conversion and matches how Tensor currently stores heterogeneous numeric types.

Value::dimension() already returns DIMENSIONLESS for Real/Int and the actual dimension for Scalar. Checking that all args share the same dimension via this method is clean and consistent with existing patterns like tensor_components_f64.

design_decisions.decision: Accept Real and Int args as dimensionless point/vector components
design_decisions.rationale: The task says 'all components must have the same dimension', not 'all components must be Scalar'. Accepting Real(1.0) for dimensionless points (e.g., unit vectors) is ergonomic and consistent with how other stdlib functions accept numeric types via as_f64.

design_decisions.decision: Tests go in the existing #[cfg(test)] module inside lib.rs, not separate test files
design_decisions.rationale: All existing stdlib tests (abs, sqrt, min, max, cross, dot, magnitude, orient, etc.) are in the inline #[cfg(test)] module. Following this convention keeps tests co-located with the implementation.

design_decisions.decision: Add Value::Point(Vec<Value>) and Value::Vector(Vec<Value>) as distinct enum variants rather than tagging Value::Tensor
design_decisions.rationale: The Type system already distinguishes Type::Point from Type::Vector. Adding distinct Value variants makes runtime values match this semantic distinction, enabling downstream code (affine transforms, geometric operations) to pattern-match on point vs vector. A boolean tag on Tensor would break every existing Tensor match arm. Separate variants let existing Tensor code remain untouched while Point/Vector patterns are added alongside.

design_decisions.decision: tensor_components_f64 accepts Point and Vector in addition to Tensor
design_decisions.rationale: Existing stdlib operations (magnitude, normalize, cross, dot, orient) use tensor_components_f64 to extract numeric components. Points and vectors should support these operations, so the helper is extended to match all three container variants.

design_decisions.decision: Arithmetic operations preserve Point/Vector variant type through the result
design_decisions.rationale: point3(1,2,3) + point3(4,5,6) should produce a Point, and vec3(1,2,3) + vec3(4,5,6) should produce a Vector. This is consistent with geometric algebra conventions and preserves semantic information through computation chains.

reuse.how: Used to extract physical dimension from each constructor arg for same-dimension validation. Returns DIMENSIONLESS for Real/Int, actual dimension for Scalar.
Used to validate that constructor args are numeric (Int, Real, or Scalar). Returns None for non-numeric types.
Used in tests to construct Scalar values with specific dimensions and validate dimension matching.
New match arms follow the existing pattern: match on function name string, validate args, return Value or Value::Undef.
Tests follow the same pattern: call eval_builtin with name and args, match on result variant, assert values.

reuse.what: Existing Value trait impl patterns (content_hash, PartialEq, Ord, Display)
reuse.how: New Value::Point/Vector arms follow the exact same patterns as Value::Tensor (tag-prefixed hash, same-variant equality, type_tag ordering, bracket-delimited display) with distinct tag bytes.
Extended to also extract from Value::Point/Vector using or-pattern matching, so all existing vector math operations work on points/vectors without further changes.
